description_long: This object appears to be a highly abstracted or heavily eroded representation of an airplane, with a generally rectangular base and several protruding, irregular forms that could correspond to the fuselage and wings. The surface is rough and granular, resembling gravel or sand, with scattered, blocky elements that might represent engines, tail, or landing gear. The overall form lacks clear aerodynamic features, with no visible cockpit, windows, or detailed control surfaces. The coloration is predominantly light gray with darker gray patches, giving it a weathered, stone-like appearance. The object is low to the ground and does not display any obvious markings or insignia. Its structure is solid and unrefined, with no visible openings or articulated parts.
description: A rough, stone-like airplane form with a granular surface and blocky protrusions, mostly gray in color. The object is abstract and lacks detailed features such as a cockpit or clear wings.
description_view_0: From this angle, the object shows a rectangular base with several upright, blocky forms, suggesting the main body and possible wing stubs.
description_view_1: This view highlights the uneven, granular surface and the scattered, irregular protrusions, with the overall shape appearing more fragmented.
description_view_2: Seen from the side, the object appears very flat and elongated, with minimal vertical features and a mostly continuous surface.
description_view_3: This perspective emphasizes the low profile and the distribution of the blocky elements, with the granular texture dominating the view.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Wings are not clearly defined; any wing-like extensions are blocky and indistinct, with no visible dihedral or sweep.
attribute_tail_design: No discernible tail configuration; rear of the object is irregular and lacks vertical or horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_cockpit_placement: No cockpit is visible or defined on the object.
